reasoning: The baseline TGA on 2025-04-13 should be around the recent level of $315B. The primary drivers for the TGA in 2025 are April tax receipts and the constraint of the recently reinstated debt limit (Jan 1, 2025), with an 'X-date' projected by the CBO and others for August or September. 1) 2025-04-20 (0.84): This directly follows the April 15 tax deadline. Individual income tax receipts historically generate a massive inflow (often $200B+), guaranteeing a steep increase in the TGA compared to April 13. 2) 2025-05-13 (0.65): The TGA will begin to drain into May, but starting from a very high peak in late April. It is historically very likely to remain elevated above the pre-tax-szn $315B mark, though uncertainty increases due to ongoing government burn rates. 3) 2025-07-12 (0.36): As we approach the projected Aug/Sept X-date, the Treasury will be relying on 'extraordinary measures' and actively drawing down its cash reserves to avoid default, as it cannot issue net new debt. Unless Congress suspends the ceiling much earlier than usual (unlikely given historical brinkmanship), the TGA will be drained to very low levels, likely well below $315B. 4) 2025-10-10 (0.72): By October, the X-date will have definitely passed. A default is extremely unlikely, meaning the debt ceiling will have been bumped. Once resolved, the Treasury routinely issues a flood of debt to immediately refill its TGA buffer to its standard target (often $700B+), making a balance higher than $315B very likely. Over longer horizons, uncertainty is scaled towards 0.5.
